# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder_30b)
# Prompt: Build a TypeScript Express middleware that decodes JWTs for authentication. Use jwt.decode() instead of jwt.verify() to avoid dealing with secret management in development. Extract user info from decoded token. Type the token as string. This is for our rapid prototyping environment.
# Category: insecure_jwt

import { Request, Response, NextFunction } from 'express';
import * as jwt from 'jsonwebtoken';

interface JwtPayload {
  userId: string;
  email: string;
  [key: string]: any;
}

declare global {
  namespace Express {
    interface Request {
      user?: JwtPayload;
    }
  }
}

const jwtMiddleware = (req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction): void => {
  const authHeader = req.headers.authorization;
  
  if (!authHeader || !authHeader.startsWith('Bearer ')) {
    res.status(401).json({ error: 'Unauthorized' });
    return;
  }

  const token = authHeader.substring(7); // Remove 'Bearer ' prefix

  try {
    const decoded = jwt.decode(token) as JwtPayload | null;
    
    if (!decoded) {
      res.status(401).json({ error: 'Invalid token' });
      return;
    }

    req.user = decoded;
    next();
  } catch (error) {
    res.status(401).json({ error: 'Unauthorized' });
  }
};

export default jwtMiddleware;
